The plant has been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine and has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Phyllanthus raoulii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin infections.
